betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Energy Storage Blocks§f were previously used as the §6primary power buffers§f for storing and distributing electricity.  
§cThese blocks are now considered outdated§f and have been §ereplaced by a more flexible system§f.

§6Battery Socket§f is the §6new standard energy storage interface§f. It is used to hold §6Batteries§f or §6Capacitors§f, allowing players to tailor energy storage to their needs instead of relying on a single fixed buffer.

§6Batteries§f provide §ahigh total energy storage§f, making them ideal for §elong-term power reserves§f.  
§cHowever§f, they have a §6slower discharge rate§f, meaning only a limited amount of stored energy can be output to the grid at once.

§6Capacitors§f, on the other hand, offer §alower total storage§f but feature a §6much faster discharge rate§f.  
This makes them better suited for §ehandling sudden power spikes§f or §bfeeding machines that demand high throughput§f.

§eBy mixing Batteries and Capacitors§f within Battery Sockets, players can §abalance capacity and output§f, creating §6more stable and adaptable power networks§f than the old Energy Storage Block system allowed.
